claim: C-Met is involved in epithelial-mesenchymal transition in tumor cells.

result.claim: C-Met is involved in epithelial-mesenchymal transition in tumor cells.
result.reasoning: The claim that C-Met is involved in epithelial-mesenchymal transition in tumor cells is supported by evidence from paper ID 13235609. This paper states that "VEGF inhibits tumor cell invasion and mesenchymal transition through a MET/VEGFR2 complex" and that "VEGF blockade restores and increases MET activity in GBM cells in a hypoxia-independent manner, while inducing a program reminiscent of epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition highlighted by a T-cadherin to N-cadherin switch and enhanced mesenchymal features." This directly indicates that increased MET activity is associated with and induces features of epithelial-to-mesenchymal transition in glioblastoma multiforme (GBM) tumor cells. While the paper focuses on GBM, the mechanism described provides strong support for the general involvement of C-Met in EMT in tumor cells.

evidence.text: Cav-1 functions as a tumor suppressor in the stromal microenvironment.
evidence.source: An Absence of Stromal Caveolin-1 Expression Predicts Early Tumor Recurrence and Poor Clinical Outcome in Human Breast Cancers

evidence.text: Disruption of Piezo1 in zebrafish leads to the formation of epithelial cell masses.
evidence.source: Crowding induces live cell extrusion to maintain homeostatic cell numbers in epithelia

evidence.text: mTOR signalling in cancer cells regulates G-CSF.
evidence.source: Oncogenic mTOR signaling recruits myeloid-derived suppressor cells to promote tumor initiation

evidence.text: ARID1A, encoding a subunit of the SWI/SNF chromatin-remodelling complex, is the most frequently mutated epigenetic regulator across all human cancers.
evidence.source: ARID1A-mutated ovarian cancers depend on HDAC6 activity

evidence.text: Down-regulation of CD44v6 in squamocellular tumors is a sign of malignant transformation of the epithelium.
evidence.source: Regulation of CD44v6-containing Isoforms during Proliferation of Normal and Malignant Epithelial Cells

evidence.text: Overexpression of MUC1 in human carcinoma cells is associated with constitutive activation of NF-κB p65.
evidence.source: MUC1 oncoprotein activates the IκB kinase β complex and constitutive NF-κB signalling

evidence.text: Norepinephrine upregulated VEGF (VEGFA) expression in breast cancer cells.
evidence.source: Adrenergic signaling promotes angiogenesis through endothelial cell-tumor cell crosstalk.

evidence.text: GEF-H1 and ECT2 are often overexpressed in cancer.
evidence.source: Role of RhoA-specific guanine exchange factors in regulation of endomitosis in megakaryocytes.

evidence.text: CD44 and CD44v6 were down-regulated in poorly differentiated tumors in primary squamocellular carcinomas of the head and neck.
evidence.source: Regulation of CD44v6-containing Isoforms during Proliferation of Normal and Malignant Epithelial Cells

evidence.text: Prostaglandin E2 (PGE2) plays an important role in immunosuppression and tumor growth.
evidence.source: Effect of Aspirin and Indomethacin on Prostaglandin E2 Synthesis in C6 Glioma Cells
